data.title: Virtus Diversified Income & Convertible Fund announces redemption of class A mandatory redeemable preferred shares
data.description:
• The Virtus Diversified Income & Convertible Fund (NYSE:ACV) (NYSE: ACV) on Friday announced the redemption of all 1,200,000 issued and outstanding Class A Mandatory Redeemable Preferred Shares (Class A MRP Shares) (CUSIP No. 01883J2#34).
  • The Class A MRP Shares will be redeemed at a cash price of $25.00 per share, plus any accrued and unpaid dividends (whether or not declared) up to, but not including, the redemption date.
  • This redemption follows the mandatory redemption date of October 2, 2025, and is being funded through a master margin loan agreement with a commercial bank.
  • Upon redemption, the Class A MRP Shares shall cease and terminate.
  • ACV -0.08% premarket to $24.2.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Riot Platforms sees 7% dip in September BTC production
data.description:
• Riot Platforms (NASDAQ:RIOT) produced 445 BTC in September 2025, down 7% from August, but up 8% year-over-year.
  • Average daily BTC production declined 4% month-over-month to 14.8 BTC/day.
  • Riot sold 465 BTC in September, generating $52.6M in net proceeds.
  • Average selling price per BTC was $113,043, down 2% from August.
  • Riot held 19,287 BTC at month-end, including 3,300 restricted BTC.
  • Deployed hash rate remained flat at 36.5 EH/s; operating hash rate rose 3% to 32.2 EH/s.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: PayPal cut to Peer Perform at Wolfe on branded growth uncertainty
data.description:
Wolfe Research downgraded shares of PayPal Holdings (NASDAQ:PYPL) to Peer Perform from Outperform, citing concerns that momentum in the company’s branded checkout business may take longer to materialize than investors expect.

Shares slipped -0.9% premarket.

Analyst Darrin Peller said that while management has made progress on initiatives such as modern checkout, Venmo integration and capital discipline, questions remain about PayPal’s ability to deliver consistent branded growth of around 7%. Wolfe highlighted that PayPal is guiding for 5% branded growth in the third quarter, with headwinds from softer consumer spending in key regions and pressure in Germany, its largest European market.

“Shares may remain rangebound until PYPL demonstrates its ability to consistently deliver branded growth, even as we acknowledge relatively sound EPS growth,” Peller wrote. He trimmed his year-end 2026 fair value estimate to a range of $70-$80, from a prior price target of $85.

The analysts said PayPal’s medium-term target of 8% to 10% branded growth by 2027 remains a “show-me story,” noting that penetration of modern checkout has yet to meaningfully move the needle. Growth in “Pay with Venmo” and buy now, pay later services will also require significant expansion to hit targets, amid rising competition.

Still, Wolfe pointed to potential upside catalysts including faster-than-expected branded acceleration, improved capital returns such as a dividend, and new initiatives in agentic commerce and stablecoins.

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Friday: AAPL, CVX, CBRL, JNJ
data.description:
Stock index futures were higher on Friday, as Wall Street is hoping to remain unfazed by the U.S. government shutdown. Here are the stocks to watch on the day:

Apple (AAPL) shares fell 1.2% in premarket trade as Jefferies downgraded the stock to Underperform from Hold citing excessive expectations on iPhones and foldable phones. The firm decreased the price target on the stock to $205.16 from $205.82. The analysts noted that demand for iPhone 17 started out only lukewarm but has grown over time.

Chevron (CVX) stock was largely flat before the opening bell as police said a massive fire that broke out at Chevron's (NYSE:CVX) El Segundo refinery near Los Angeles late Thursday has been contained. It is not yet clear what caused the explosion at the facility, which supplies jet fuel for the Los Angeles International Airport and supplies 20% of all motor vehicle fuels and 40% of the jet fuel consumed in Southern California.

Cracker Barrel (CBRL) shares were 1% higher in extended trade as the firm ended its relationship with the marketing firm it hired to refresh its logo and soften its rustic identity. It has ended its engagement with Prophet, the consultancy that advised the company on its previous brand refresh initiatives, which included new menu offerings as well as store and restaurant redesigns. The most controversial, however, was the new Cracker Barrel logo, which removed the familiar old man and barrel for a “cleaner version.”

Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) rose 0.8% in premarket trade as Wells Fargo upgraded the firm to overweight from equal weight, saying that the healthcare giant is attractively priced with pharmaceutical tariff risks and drug pricing concerns in the rearview mirror. The bank upped its price target to $212 from $170.

data.title: Apple dips after rating cut at Jefferies on 'excessive expectations' on iPhone
data.description:
Shares of Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) fell about 1% premarket on Friday after Jefferies downgraded the stock to Underperform from Hold citing excessive expectations on iPhones and foldable phones.

The firm decreased the price target on the stock to $205.16 from $205.82. 

"Better demand for iPhone 17, partly due to a price cut on the base model, is already in the price. That has led to excessive expectations on 18 Fold, and the replacement cycle," said analysts led by Edison Lee.

The analysts noted that demand for iPhone 17 started out only lukewarm, but has grown over time. As of today, iPhone 17 Pro Max's resale prices are still at a premium of 5% to 15% for all variants, versus only one variant trading at a premium last year.

The analysts said they attribute the better demand to — no price hike for Pro/Pro Max and an effective price cut for the base; and high trade-in value for 15/16.

Lee and his team noted that they see strong China demand likely driven by — deeper price cut at the base model than outside China, with the 256GB variant qualified for governement subsidy of RMB 500; and highest trade-in value offered for iPhone 15/16 among major markets.

The analysts said that another potential reason for higher year-over-year lead time and resale premium could be the early production start last year, which led to less shortage than this year.

"As we expected, 17 Air is the least popular by our tracking. This new form factor has not helped AAPL driven iPhone sales," said the analysts.

The analysts added that more positive sales momentum has inflated expectations on the replacement cycle and prospects of the 18 Fold.

The big debates seem to be around — speed of replacement cycle; and upside for foldable, according to the analysts.

"Without innovative features, price-driven replacement cycle may not be sustainable (could result in margin pressure). The thin form factor is fact is not popular, making any bullish view on fordable risky. We do not doubt AAPL will be able to make the most beautiful foldable phone in the market, but the question is the TAM of a US$2K phone," said Lee and his team.

The analysts noted that the most important component of the fordable — the display — comes from Samsung. Samsung's recently launched Galaxy Z Fold 7 has a display that is nearly crease-free, and its thickness (unfolded) at 4.2mm is even thinner than the 17 Air at 5.5mm. Its selling price is U.S. $2,000.

"Review and reception seems strong, but even so industry expectations for its annual vol is no more than 3m units (Fold 6's vol is 2.2m). Therefore, we believe it is the price point that limits the TAM, not the form factor," said Lee and his team.

data.title: Intercontinental Exchange reports September statistics
data.description:
Intercontinental Exchange (NYSE:ICE)  reported on Friday, September 2025 trading volume and related revenue statistics. 

September highlights include:

-Total open interest (OI) up 15% y/y, including record futures of 56.9M lots on September 25

-Energy OI up 8% y/y, including record futures of 41.0M lots on September 25

-Cotton OI up 21% y/y

-Financials OI up 34% y/y, including record futures of 14.8M lots on September 12

-NYSE Cash Equities ADV up 49% y/y

-NYSE Equity Options ADV up 17% y/y

data.title: What investing themes will be 'in' and 'out' in the second half of the decade?
data.description:
BofA is predicting what investing themes will be "in" and what will be "out" in the second half of the this decade.

For those old enough, this exercise by strategist Michael Hartnett in his weekly Flow Show note may bring back memories of Mad Magazine's Overrated & Underrated lists. 

The second half of the 2020s tilts toward security, state intervention, demographics/tech shifts, and new asset plays — while globalization, Fed independence, and old market darlings are seen as past their peak.

Humans are "out" and robots are "in." Fed independence gives way to Fed deference, quantitative easing is "out" and yield curve control is "in," the AI bull becomes the AI bubble and The Magnificent 7 (AAPL) (AMZN) (GOOG) (GOOGL) (META) (MSFT) (NVDA) (TSLA) make way for China's BATX (BIDU) (BABA) (OTCPK:TCEHY) (OTCPK:XIACY).

data.title: Unusual Machines secures $800,000 order for drone components from Red Cat
data.description:
• Unusual Machines (NYSE:UMAC) on Friday announced it has received an $800,000 order for drone components from Red Cat Holdings (RCAT).
  • The order includes Unusual Machines BLUE UAS listed Aura Analog Camera, Aura VTX, Brave Flight Controller, Brave ESC, HDO+ Goggles, and motors that will be integrated into Red Cat's FANG drones.
  • UMAC +6.34% premarket to $15.92.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Cracker Barrel cuts ties with marketing firm behind failed rebranding
data.description:
In another step to backpedal from its controversial rebranding, Cracker Barrel (NASDAQ:CBRL) has ended its relationship with the marketing firm it hired to refresh its logo and soften its rustic identity.

Cracker Barrel (NASDAQ:CBRL) said Thursday it has ended its engagement with Prophet, the consultancy that advised the company on its previous brand refresh initiatives which included new menu offerings as well as store and restaurant redesigns.

The most controversial, however, was the new Cracker Barrel logo which removed the familiar old man and barrel to a “cleaner version.”

The campaign was a strategy from new CEO Julie Felss Masino to “celebrate the diversity of all our guests with a logo that represented our continued passion for pleasing people of all races, colors, and genders.”

The strategy faced sharp criticism from customers and a swift sell-off by investors, wiping out 30% of the company’s share price in just a month. Following public rebuke from President Trump, management quickly reversed course, reinstated the original logo, and acknowledged the backlash.

"If the last few days have shown us anything, it's how deeply people care about Cracker Barrel," the company said. "You've also shown us that we could've done a better job sharing who we are and who we’ll always be."

In its latest move to lean into the nostalgia that defines Cracker Barrel, the company announced it is retooling its leadership structure by eliminating the role of chief restaurant and retail operations officer, expanding the role of vice president for field operations to an expanded store operations role, and bringing back Thomas Yun to run the company’s restaurant strategy.

More crucially, Cracker Barrel is cutting ties with Prophet.

“These changes to our organizational structure, along with new leadership appointments and promotions, mark a strategic step forward as we sharpen our focus on consistently craveable food and warm country hospitality,” Masino said on the company’s website.

Shares are higher ahead of Friday’s open.

data.title: White Mountains sells controlling stake in Bamboo to CVC Capital Partners
data.description:
• White Mountains (NYSE:WTM) to sell controlling interest in Bamboo to CVC Capital Partners.
  • The deal values Bamboo at $1.75B.
  • White Mountains expects ~$840Min net cash proceeds and a $310 gain to book value per share.
  • White Mountains will retain a 15% fully-diluted equity stake in Bamboo, valued at $250M.
  • Bamboo operates in the homeowners' insurance market in California and Texas.
  • Transaction expected to close by end of Q4 2025.
  • Source: Press release
